field_values.action_summary: A ritualized "capture" takes place as three-to-four-foot-tall Martians emerge from the incubator. Women and youths form a gauntlet to collect the newly hatched beings. The scene highlights the communal, non-parental nature of their upbringing.
field_values.camera_intent: Close-ups of cracking eggs and emerging hatchlings; Mid-shots of the gauntlet of women/youths catching Martians; Handheld/unsteady shots capturing frantic capture energy
field_values.end_state: scene resolves into the next narrative beat
movement_notes: Physical size of Newly Hatched Martians (3 to 4 feet tall)
Movement patterns and rhythm of the gauntlet
field_values.transition_notes:
# Scene Contract: CH007_SC002

## Production Intent
Establish the pitiless and communal biological cycle of Mars through a ritualized capture event. The scene must emphasize that Martian upbringing is a collective social function rather than a parental one, using the frantic energy of the hatchlings to drive the tension.

## Required Cast
- protagonist
- sola
- Newly Hatched Martians (3-4ft tall)
- Martian women/youths

## Required Environments
- the_incubator_enclosure (walled enclosure with egg clusters and gauntlet path)

## Continuity Constraints
- Hatchling height must remain consistent at 3 to 4 feet.
- The movement of the Martian women/youths must follow a specific ritualized gauntlet pattern.

## Beat List
- BT001: The cracking of the eggs (Anticipation)
- BT002: The emergence of the hatchlings (Transition to Intensity)
- BT003: The ritualized capture gauntlet (Clinical/Ritualistic Intensity)
field_values.dialogue_relevance: dialogue supports scene intent and continuity
